page: Vultr vhf-3c-8gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 3 shared x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 3 physical cores with 1 thread per core, 8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.67 GB per core, and a bundled 256 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. The processor features 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instructions.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2401.69 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching a top-tier 38.09 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 7120.84 and PassMark CPU scoring 5738.4. Memory performance shows a clear tradeoff: cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 395.59 GB/sec, but large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the bottom 25% at 32.54 GB/sec and 16.94 GB/sec respectively.

Given the shared CPU allocation and moderate multi-core capabilities, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that benefit from high single-thread execution speeds but do not require sustained, heavy multi-core utilization. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves average speeds of 145.15 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. It is less optimal for memory-intensive applications or larger machine learning models due to uncached memory bandwidth limitations.
description: Vultr vhf-3c-8gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 3 shared x86_64 vCPUs on an Intel Skylake architecture running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 256 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It exhibits strong single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 2401.69 and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression of 38.09 GB/sec. However, multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth benchmarks are weaker, with large-block write bandwidth limited to 16.94 GB/sec and PassMark CPU score at 5738.4. This resource profile makes the server a cost-efficient option for workloads prioritizing single-thread speed over massive parallel processing, such as lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Vultr vhf-3c-8gb is a High Frequency Compute virtual server configured with 3 shared Intel vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 256 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance.

tagline: A shared-CPU virtual instance featuring strong single-core performance, balanced memory, and integrated high-speed NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Three shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 256 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core performance and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression
Moderate multi-core capabilities and lower uncached memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and small LLM inference
